channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario planning to crack down on ticket reselling for large events such as FIFA World Cup
Ontario Liberal interim leader says nomination was fair, despite candidate's comments
item.description: The interim leader of the Ontario Liberal Party says a nomination race this weekend to select a byelection candidate was fair, open and transparent, despite suspicion being raised by Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith.

item.title: Wait times at Ontario emergency rooms have spiked over past 5 years, study says
item.description: Wait times for people seeking care at emergency departments across Ontario have dramatically increased over the past five years amid a “deepening Ontario hospital funding crisis,” according to a new report.

item.title: Ontario nurses launch constitutional challenge over lack of right to strike
item.description: The Ontario Nurses’ Association is launching a constitutional challenge of a law that prevents them from taking any form of job action during the bargaining process.

item.title: Ontario pharmacists now allowed to treat these 9 conditions
item.description: Ontario pharmacists will soon be allowed to administer more vaccines and treat more common ailments, as part of the government’s continuing moves to expand their scope of practice.

item.title: Liberal MP Erskine-Smith loses nomination battle for Ontario provincial byelection
item.description: Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith has lost a provincial nomination race, failing to clear the first of several hurdles toward the leadership of the Ontario Liberals.

item.title: Ontario Liberal MPP from Etobicoke enters leadership race
item.description: Ontario Liberal legislator Lee Fairclough has announced her bid for the party’s leadership.

item.title: Ontario Liberals set to pick byelection candidate amid jabs in nomination race
item.description: An Ontario Liberal nomination race that has seen some candidates trade jabs and question others’ community bona fides is set to culminate Saturday with members selecting their pick for an upcoming byelection.

item.title: World’s largest nuclear facility could be built in Ontario with new $300M deal
item.description: The Ontario government is moving ahead on a cost-sharing agreement worth up to $300 million to advance the construction of a new nuclear generating station at the Bruce Power site in Kincardine, Ont.

item.title: Ontario to lower age for colorectal cancer screening
item.description: New guidelines published online by Cancer Care Ontario say the province is lowering its colorectal cancer screening age from 50 to 45 in response to a “notable” increase in younger people diagnosed with the disease.

item.title: Ford criticizes Stiles for ‘Trump-style rhetoric’ as she accuses government of ‘clutching its pearls’ over prison comment
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford accused Opposition Leader Marit Stiles Tuesday of “stepping over a line” with a comment about how he could end up “in jail” if an inquiry were called into the Greenbelt.

item.title: ‘People are fed up with the home invasions:’ Doug Ford says feds taking too long on bail reform bill
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday he’s frustrated at the pace of a federal bill meant to toughen up bail laws.

item.title: Data show racial, language disparities in mandatory Ontario teacher math test
item.description: Results of a math test teacher candidates in Ontario must pass in order to be certified show striking disparities across language, age and racial groups, the province’s teachers’ federation says.

item.title: Premier Doug Ford receives honorary degree from U.S. university
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford received an honorary doctorate from a U.S. university on Saturday.

item.title: Ontario opposition leader calls on Carney to block Ford government’s airport plans
item.description: Ontario NDP Leader Marit Stiles is calling on Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government to block Ontario’s plans around the expansion of Billy Bishop Airport in downtown Toronto, saying she believes the move could result in wider destruction of the Toronto islands.
